page_subtitle: Everything you need to know about racing, coaching and gate nights at the Eagles Nest.
no_results_text: No questions matched. Try different words, or email us at eastfieldeagles@gmail.com.
chat_panel_subtitle: Tap a topic or search to find answers.
chat_unanswered_message: I don't have an answer for that one. Email us at eastfieldeagles@gmail.com and we'll get back to you.

questions.question: How do I start riding with the Eastfield Eagles?
questions.answer: Easiest way: come down to the track on a Tuesday Gate Night from 6:00pm. Bring a bike, helmet, long sleeves and long pants. Your first 4 weeks are free — no licence required to try us out.

questions.question: Do I need to be a member to come to gate night?
questions.answer: Not initially — your first 4 weeks are free for anyone interested. After that you'll need an AUS Cycling licence to keep riding regularly. We'll help you sort it out.

questions.question: What's the minimum age to ride?
questions.answer: We have riders as young as 2 in our Mini-Wheelers programme. There's no upper age limit — we have plenty of riders in their 50s and 60s.

questions.question: Where is the Eagles Nest?
questions.answer: 195 Colchester Road, Kilsyth VIC 3137. Plenty of parking on site. The track is signposted from the main road.

questions.question: How much does Gate Night cost?
questions.answer: $3 per rider for AUS Cycling members. Your first 4 weeks are free if you're new to the club.

questions.question: How much is coaching?
questions.answer: Coaching with Connor on Tuesdays runs from $5 to $20 per session depending on the stream (Mini-Wheelers, Beginner or Intermediate). Wednesday All-Abilities coaching with Harry is included for members.

questions.question: Do I need an AUS Cycling licence?
questions.answer: Yes, after your first 4 free weeks. AUS Cycling licences are required for all racing and ongoing track access. You can sign up at auscycling.org.au — there are options for casual riders, juniors and full racing licences.

questions.question: What's included in club membership?
questions.answer: Track access, gate night participation, Wednesday all-abilities coaching, club racing events, member-only events and the Eagles community. Talk to us at the canteen for current membership rates.

questions.question: What gear do I need to ride?
questions.answer: Minimum: a bike, a helmet (full-face preferred for racing, open-face fine for starting out), long sleeves and long pants. Closed-toe shoes. Gloves are strongly recommended. We can sometimes lend gear to brand-new riders for their first session.

questions.question: Do I need a BMX-specific bike?
questions.answer: For first-timers, any bike that fits and isn't a road bike will get you through gate night. If you decide to stick with it, you'll want a proper BMX race bike — we can advise on what to look for and where to buy.

questions.question: Can I borrow a bike?
questions.answer: We have a small number of loan bikes for first-time riders to try out. Mention it when you arrive at gate night and we'll see what's available.

questions.question: What number do I pick for racing?
questions.answer: Anything between 8 and 999. Once it's yours and registered, no one else in the state can take it.

questions.question: When are races held?
questions.answer: Club racing happens periodically throughout the season — check our Facebook page or EntryBoss for the next race calendar. Gate nights every Tuesday are practice/training rather than racing.

questions.question: Can my child race at other clubs?
questions.answer: Absolutely — that's the point of an AUS Cycling licence. They can race at any AUS Cycling-affiliated BMX track in Australia.

questions.question: How do I enter a race?
questions.answer: Most races run through EntryBoss (entryboss.cc). Once you have an AUS Cycling licence, you can register for any open event.

questions.question: Has the club produced any top riders?
questions.answer: Yes — we've sent kids as young as 9 to the World Championships and produced top-8 Australian and Victorian riders. The track and coaches have a strong pedigree.

questions.question: When was the club founded?
questions.answer: Maroondah BMX Club — the Eastfield Eagles — was founded in 1980 at Norton Park, Croydon. We moved to the current home on Colchester Road, Kilsyth in 1991, and reopened in January 2025 with a brand new Pro Gate and a transformed track.

questions.question: Who runs the club?
questions.answer: We're a volunteer-run, member-owned club. Committee members are all parents, riders and ex-riders who do this for love of the sport. If you'd like to get involved, talk to us at the canteen.

questions.question: How can I volunteer or help out?
questions.answer: Easiest way is to come along regularly and chat with the committee. We always need hands for canteen, race-day setup, coaching support and event running. Even an hour a month makes a difference.
